explanation: This command uses the standard -H flag to set a custom User-Agent header, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It is a direct representation of the behavior the rule is designed to detect.
This command uses the long-form equivalent of the -H flag (--header) to set the User-Agent header. This is a valid substitution technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses single quotes instead of double quotes to define the header string. This is a safe mutation via substitution and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds an additional -o /dev/null flag to discard the output, which does not affect the detection logic. It is a safe mutation via insertion and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses a normalized path for curl.exe (e.g., without specifying the full path), which is a safe mutation via omission and still matches the Sigma rule.
